1.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
1.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);

3.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

6.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
6.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

7.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

11.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
11.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

11.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

12.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
12.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

12.gl: a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

15.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
15.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

16.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
16.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

17.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

18.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
18.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)
